card.intuition: UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) models the high-d data as a weighted k-nearest-neighbour graph (fuzzy simplicial set), then optimises low-d embeddings to reproduce the same topology. High-d similarities: p_ij = fuzzy membership based on local radius ρᵢ (distance to nearest neighbour). Low-d repulsion/attraction: minimise cross-entropy between high-d and low-d graphs using SGD.
card.analogy: Making a flat map of a globe: you can't perfectly flatten a sphere without distortion, but UMAP finds the flat layout that best preserves neighbourhood relationships. Points that are close together on the globe stay close on the map — UMAP preserves local structure globally much better than t-SNE.
card.time_complexity: O(n · k · d) for kNN graph; O(n · epochs) for embedding optimisation
card.space_complexity: O(n · k) for the kNN graph
when_to_use: Visualising high-dimensional data (embeddings, single-cell RNA-seq) in 2D/3D
Dimensionality reduction as pre-processing for clustering or classification
Faster and more scalable than t-SNE; better global structure preservation
gotchas: n_neighbors controls local/global trade-off — small (5-15): tight local clusters; large (50-200): global structure
min_dist controls cluster compactness — 0 for tightest clusters, 0.5 for dispersed
UMAP is non-deterministic (random init) — set random_state for reproducibility

exercise.prompt: Implement the UMAP fuzzy membership function for a single point's neighbourhood.
exercise.starter_code:
import math

def umap_membership(distances, rho, sigma):
    """
    Compute fuzzy membership values for a point's k neighbours.
    UMAP high-d similarity: v_{ij} = exp(-(d_{ij} - rho) / sigma)
    Clamped to [0, 1]; for the nearest neighbour (d=rho): v=1.
    distances: sorted distances to k neighbours (ascending)
    rho      : distance to the nearest neighbour (= distances[0])
    sigma    : bandwidth parameter (solved via binary search in full UMAP)
    Returns list of membership values.
    """
    return [
        ___________
        for d in distances
    ]
hints: UMAP membership: exp(-(d - ρ) / σ), clamped to [0,1]. When d = ρ (nearest neighbour), the exponent is 0 so membership = 1.
exercise.test_code:
import math

dists = [0.5, 0.8, 1.2, 2.0]  # sorted distances
rho   = 0.5  # nearest neighbour distance
sigma = 0.5

memberships = umap_membership(dists, rho, sigma)

# Nearest neighbour: membership = 1
assert abs(memberships[0] - 1.0) < 1e-9
# Membership decreases with distance
assert memberships[0] >= memberships[1] >= memberships[2] >= memberships[3]
# All values in [0, 1]
assert all(0 <= m <= 1 for m in memberships)
# No negative values (clamp)
dists2 = [0.1, 0.5]
memb2 = umap_membership(dists2, rho=0.5, sigma=0.3)
assert memb2[0] == 0.0  # d < rho -> clamped to 0
print('PASS')
